spec_match.rationale: Uses `caret-animation: manual | auto` with an `@keyframes` colour cycle on `caret-color`, exactly the pattern in the css-ui-4 spec.

interactive.rationale: Two real buttons toggling between auto/manual, with a live editable input that the visitor can click into to see the caret behaviour change.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Blurb promises a toggle between platform default and author-driven animation; the page delivers exactly that.

edge_cases.rationale: Doesn't cover prefers-reduced-motion override, what happens with caret-shape, or focus loss while animating.

references.rationale: Includes the correct chromestatus.com/feature/5082469066604544 link in see-also.

design_system.rationale: Variables only, keyframe colours come from --accent-rose/--accent-blue/--accent-emerald, no static-card.

openQuestions.title: Show prefers-reduced-motion interaction
openQuestions.detail: The accessibility motivation is the spec's central justification. Pair the toggle with a 'simulate prefers-reduced-motion' switch and demonstrate that the keyframes opt-out gracefully via @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce).

openQuestions.title: Demonstrate animation primitives beyond keyframes
openQuestions.detail: `caret-animation: manual` also unlocks transitions and Web Animations API. The flagship demo should at least mention this; the transition-vs-keyframes sibling covers it but this primary page leaves visitors thinking it's keyframes-only.

summary: Canonical toggle demo. Strong on spec match. Still missing the accessibility-overrides path that the spec calls out as a primary motivation.
